name: Pipe Dream | A B2B Marketing Podcast
description:
Pipe Dream profiles the B2B companies that have cracked the code on audience-driven marketing, building podcasts, newsletters, and content channels that don't just generate awareness, but actually drive qualified pipeline.
Host Jason Bradwell, founder of B2B Better, sits down with the marketers and founders who've shifted away from traditional demand gen tactics to build their own audiences. Each episode breaks down the numbers, the conversion systems, and the tactical decisions that turn listen

recent_episodes.title: How One Founder Got 50 Customers in 60 Days Using LinkedIn Organic | Adam Holmgren, CEO and Co-Founder at Fibbler
recent_episodes.description:
We help B2B brands launch shows that turn their point of view into pipeline. If you're launching a podcast (or have one already) and are not sure how it can hit your bottom line, book a meeting with Jason: https://meetings-eu1.hubspot.com/jason-bradwell/youtube-meeting-link

--

Adam Holmgren turned three years of consistent LinkedIn posting into a $700K ARR SaaS business—without cold outbound, without a sales team, and with 80% of growth coming from organic content amplified by t

recent_episodes.title: If You Can Do Everything You Are Nothing for Nobody | Jason Bradwell, Founder of B2B Better and Host of Pipe Dream Podcast
recent_episodes.description:
This episode is brought to you by B2B Better. We turned down SaaS clients and e-commerce brands to become the only video-first podcast agency for service-based B2B businesses. Specificity is the strategy. 

If you've ever said "we can do that" to every client who walks in, this episode is your wake-up call. Host Jason Bradwell breaks down why niching down is the fastest path to becoming the obvious choice, and why being a bit of everything for everyone means you're actually nothing for

recent_episodes.title: Four Questions That Will Tell You If Your Podcast Is Dead | Jason Bradwell, Founder of B2B Better and Host of Pipe Dream Podcast
recent_episodes.description:
This episode is brought to you by B2B Better. Most owned media audits produce 50-page reports with vague recommendations and zero next steps. We give you four questions, 90 minutes, and a clear decision: kill, fix, or scale. 

If your podcast has downloads but no pipeline, this episode shows you how to audit your entire owned media strategy in 90 minutes and walk away knowing what's broken and how to fix it. Host Jason Bradwell breaks down the Four R Framework — Reach, Resonance, Revenu

score_reason: Focuses on audience-driven marketing and content channels for B2B, but less emphasis on AI strategy or traditional industry transformation.

key_themes: Owned media as pipeline driver (podcasts, newsletters, content channels that convert)
Moving away from traditional demand gen and rented channels toward audience-first strategy
Proving marketing ROI and connecting content to revenue, not just vanity metrics
B2B positioning, differentiation, and thought leadership through consistent point of view
Building systems and frameworks that scale content creation without burning out teams
enrichment.relevant_episode: Why Your Podcast Has Downloads But No Pipeline — Andreas built a business in one of the most traditional industries on earth and can speak to why most owned media fails commercially and how to fix it, moving from downloads to actual revenue
enrichment.coverage_gap: How traditional, non-tech industries (food, agriculture, manufacturing) are using AI and data to reshape their competitive positioning and audience strategy — the podcast has covered B2B tech, SaaS, and services, but not commodity/traditional industries awakening to owned media
enrichment.suggested_hook: The guy who sold Canada's largest food agency now teaches AI strategy at Ivey — here's why traditional industries are about to out-compete tech companies at the one thing tech got right: turning data into decisions
